content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcel PnecrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April26,2004
Michael R.W. Houston, Esq.
Rutan & Tucker, LLP
Post Office Box 1950
Costa Mesa, CA y2628-1950
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-026
Dear Mr. Houston
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of La Quinta City Councilman Lee
Osborne for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act').I Nothing in this letter should be constnred to evaluate any conduct that has already
taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices
Commission (the "Commission') does not act as the finder of fact when it renders advice. (In
re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.)
QUESTTON
Do the Act's prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply if
Councilmember Osborne's accountancy firm places an advertisement in a chamber of
cornmerce newsletter, funded in part by city funds, when the advertisement includes a
photograph of the official and his firm name includes the name "osborne"?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
FACTS
The La Quinta Chamber of Commerce (the Chamber) publishes a newsletter ten
months of every year. This newsletter is mailed free of charge to every resident, business
and Chamber member in La Quinta by presorted standard rnail delivery. The newsletter is
I Government Code sections 8 I 000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8109- I 8997, of the California Code of Regulations. All statutory references herein are to the Government
Code unless otherwise indicated. A1l regulatory references herein are to Title 2 of the California Code of
Regulations unless otherwise indicated.
l::+epl


File No. A-04-026
Page No. 2
also mailed on a subscription basis for $25 per year to those not included in the free
distribution area.
The City of La Quinta pays the Chamber a yearly amount of $125,000. The Chamber
uses this payment for several services: (l) assistance with business retention and growth, (2)
operations, and (3) to produce four pages in each edition of the newsletter to post city notices
and information.
Councilrnember Osbome owns a certified public accountancy corporation operating
in the City of La Quinta and services clients throughout the area. Councilmernber Osborne's
firm would like to place an advertisement in the newsletter. The firm would pay firm market
value for the advertisement-
The advertisement would not identifu Councilmernber Osbome as a member of the
La Quinta City Council. Rather, theproposed advertisement would exclusivelyprovide
information about the firm's business. The firm proposes to include the name of the firm -
'?eterson Slater Osborne" which includes Councilmember Osborne's last name. The council
member's name would appear in the same manner as the other names in the firm's title.
Additionally, the firm proposes to include a picture of the firm's entire staf{ which includes
approximately twelve people. The current proposal includes Councilmember Osbome in the
picture.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 states that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense." section 82041.5 defines a "mass mailing" as "overtwo hundred
substantially similar pieces of mail, but does not include a form letter or other mail sent in
response to an unsolicited request, letter or other inquiry."
Regulation 18901(a) states that "[e]xcept as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by section 89001 if all of the following criteria are met:
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by anymeans, to the recipient at his or
her residence, place of ernployment or business, or post office box ....
"(2) The item sent either:
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the
mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected officer.


File No. A-04-026
Page No. 3
"(3XA) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys;
or
"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00 are
paid with public moneys, and the design, production, orprinting is
done with the intent of sending the item otherthan as permitted by this
regulation.
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a
singular calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).,,
(Emphasis added.)
Your facts indicate that the newsletter will be delivered to recipients at the
recipient's residencg place of employment, business, orpost officebox and that more
than 200 will be sent in a calendarmonth. Because there is no subscription list for
these recipients, the newsletter does not fit within the'bnsolicited request,, definition.
You have also stated that the newsletter is paid for, in part, with public funds
because a portion of the annual $125,000 payment made fromthe City ol1-a euinta
to the chamber pays for the costs of production and distibution we have
consistently advised that a newsletter which is circulated and distributed in part with
public funds falls within the Act's mass mailing prohibition under section Sb00f .
(Jenkins Advice Letter, A-01-213:' Moore Advice Letter, A-99-234; BattersbyAdvice
Letter, A-94-303).
Since the newsletter meets the three criteria under l s90l(a)(l ,3, & 4), your
request for advice concerns the only remaining criterion set forth under lS90liaX2).
Therefore, if the newsletter "[{features an elected officer affiliated with the agency,
or "[i]ncludes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected offi"o
affiliated with the agency ... and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected fficef'[ernphasis added] it meets all the
criteria set forth under regulation l s90l(a)(2XA&B) and is a prohibited mass
mailing.
Regulation 18901(c)(2) states that an item "'[f]eatures an elected officer'... [if
itJ includes the elected officer's photograph ... or singles out the elected officer by
the manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of the document ....,,
(Emphasis added.) Because the advertisernent includes a jhotograph of
Councilmernber Osborne, it "features an elected officer" and, therefore meets the
meets the criterion under regulation 18901(a)(2)(A).
Additionally, since the newsletter "is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with" Councilmember Osborne, and includes
both the nilme and photograph of Councilmember Osbome, it also meets the criterion
established under regulation 1 S90 I (a)(2)(B).


File No. A-04-026
Page No. 4
Your letter suggests that the name and picture of Councilmember Osborne in
the advertisernent is not linked or "affiliated" with the City of La Quint4 because it
makes no reference to Councilmember Osborne in his position as an elected official,
in that it only includes the council member's last name as part of the firm name -
Peterson ' Slater ' Osborne. Given this fact, your request asks if the single mention of
Councilmember Osbome's last name as part of a firm nzrme, and his inclusion in a
group photograph, with no reference to his position as an elected officer, is enough to
satisff the requirernents of "[i]ncludes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agencf provision under regulation
18e01(a)(2)(B.)
With respect to the issue of whether or not the inclusion of an elected officer's
last name in the name of a firm is enough to bring the item within the mass mailing
provisions of regulation 18901(a)(2), the literal language of the regulation appears to
prohibit this. With respect to an item that includes aphotograph of the elected officer
without identification, we have advised that consistent with the provisions of
regulation 18901 aphotograph is prohibited, even when the elected official is not
identified. (Smith Advice Letter, No. I-97-549.) This includes group photographs
(Fisicaro Advice Letter No. A-96-230).
Therefore, we conclude that any inclusion of the name (including last name)
orphotograph of an elected officer in apublicly funded mass mailing would be a
prohibited mass mailing.
Finally, the Commission is currently reviewing possible modifications to the
mass mailing regulations and will be conducting an'lnterested persons meeting" in
the near future. If you would like to be included in this process, and have any input
that you would like to be considered with respect to these issues, please let me know.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
William J. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division
wJLjg

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the Act's prohibitions against mass mailings at public expense apply if
Councilmember Osborne's accountancy firm places an advertisement in a chamber of
cornmerce newsletter, funded in part by city funds, when the advertisement includes a
photograph of the official and his firm name includes the name "osborne"?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
